subtitle: Back of thigh — common in speed work and late in races

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Hamstring strains peak during speed sessions and the final km of long runs

mild.description: Tightness or ache in the back of the thigh, eases with warm-up
mild.can_run_note: Yes — no speed work or hill sprints until resolved
why: Hamstring tightness from repeated hip flexion without adequate stretching
Fatigue-related tightness in the final km of long runs
Anterior pelvic tilt increasing hamstring length demand
relief: Standing hamstring stretch with foot on a surface at hip height 60s each side
Foam roll hamstrings slowly from knee to glute
Easy cycling 20 min to loosen hamstrings without load
prevention: Nordic hamstring curls — the single best hamstring injury prevention exercise
Hip flexor stretching to reduce anterior pelvic tilt
Glute strengthening to take load off hamstrings
Dynamic hamstring warm-up before speed sessions

moderate.description: Sharp pulling sensation during runs, especially at faster paces
moderate.can_run_note: Easy conversational pace only — zero speed work
why: Proximal hamstring tendinopathy at the ischial tuberosity
Partial hamstring strain with ongoing micro-tearing
Sciatic nerve irritation mimicking hamstring pain
relief: Avoid stretching a strained hamstring — load it instead
Isometric hamstring holds: seated leg curl hold 45s × 5
Reduce mileage by 50% — eliminate all fast running
prevention: Avoid sustained hip flexion positions that stress the proximal tendon

severe.description: Acute tearing pain mid-run, unable to continue, bruising developing
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible significant hamstring tear
why: Grade 2 or 3 hamstring strain requiring grading via ultrasound
Proximal hamstring avulsion in severe cases
Sciatic nerve involvement needing specialist assessment
relief: Crutches if unable to walk normally
Imaging to confirm grade before starting rehabilitation
prevention: Return to running protocol: 6 weeks minimum for grade 2
Full sprint capacity required before race participation

see_physio_if: Bruising appearing down the back of the thigh
Sharp proximal pain at the sitting bone
Symptoms down the leg to the foot suggesting nerve involvement
